scope: All areas of civil engineering including structural engineering, geotechnical engineering, transportation engineering, water resources engineering, environmental engineering, construction management, surveying and geo-spatial engineering, tunnel engineering, coastal and harbor engineering, earthquake engineering, bridge engineering, AI and smart infrastructure. Open to Korean and international researchers.

manuscript_structure.abstract_note: Should state briefly the purpose, methods, principal results, and major conclusions. Avoid references and non-standard abbreviations.
manuscript_structure.keywords_note: Select terms that best represent the content. Avoid terms already used in the title.

formatting.file_format: Word (.doc/.docx) or LaTeX (Springer article class). PDF for review only.

references.format: Numbered in order of appearance, cited as [1], [2], etc.
examples: [1] Park, R. and Paulay, T. (1975). Reinforced Concrete Structures, John Wiley & Sons, New York, NY, USA.

[2] Kim, S., Lee, J., and Park, H. (2023). "Machine learning-based seismic damage prediction for RC buildings." KSCE Journal of Civil Engineering, Vol. 27, No. 5, pp. 2145-2158, DOI: 10.1007/s12205-023-1234-5.
[3] ACI Committee 318 (2019). Building Code Requirements for Structural Concrete (ACI 318-19), American Concrete Institute, Farmington Hills, MI, USA.
references.formatting_rules: Author surname then initials. Year in parentheses. Article titles in double quotes. Journal names in italics. Volume and pages required. DOI required where available.

figures_tables.placement: embedded in text or at end of manuscript
figures_tables.color_note: Color figures available online. Additional charges may apply for color in print.

submission_notes.equations: Numbered consecutively in parentheses at right margin
submission_notes.math_notation: MathType or Word equation editor, or LaTeX math environments
submission_notes.ethical_requirements: Original unpublished work, not under review elsewhere. Conflict of interest statement required.
